statement.p: Town A and Town B are 330 miles apart. A boat traveled from Town A to Town B, and then back to Town A. Since the river flows from Town B to Town A, the boats speed was 25 miles per hour faster when it traveled from Town B to Town A. The whole trip took 11 hours. Answer the following questions:
The boat traveled from Town A to Town B at the speed of miles per hour.
The boat traveled from Town B back to Town A at the speed of miles per hour.

solution.p: Assume that the boat traveled from Town A to Town B at x miles per hour.
Then, it traveled from Town B back to Town A at x+25 miles per hour.
Using the formula time=\frac{distance}{speed}, we know the boat traveled from Town A to Town B in \frac{330}{x} hours; and it traveled back from Town B to Town A in \frac{330}{x+25} hours.
Its given that the whole trip took 11 hours, so we can write an equation and solve it:
When numbers are big, we use the quadratic formula to solve the quadratic equation:
In this situation, x=-15 is not a valid solution, since speed cannot be negative, x=50 is the only solution. The boat traveled from Town A to Town B at the speed of {50} miles per hour.
The boat traveled from Town B back to Town A at the speed of {50}+25={75} miles per hour.
